summary: As a breakthrough proves elusive in the quest to deploy the nascent technology and boost earnings, global finance is divided on how to proceed.

summary: April’s rally in risk assets has weathered stalled peace talks, signs of resurgent inflation and a central-bank succession drama. Investors may be about to find out if the speed of the advance itself poses a threat to a further ascent.

title: The Stock Picking Philosophy to Find the Next Amazon: Masters in Business

They discuss David's new book "Rule Breaker Investing: How to Pick the Best Stocks of the Future and Build Lasting Wealth." They also discuss the pros and cons of different investing strategies and trends. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Bloomberg This Weekend 4/26/2026
title_cn: 彭博社本周末 2026 年 4 月 26 日
summary: The news doesn’t stop when markets close. Hosts David Gura, Christina Ruffini and Lisa Mateo bring clarity, context and a bit of humor to the weekend’s biggest headlines, LIVE from New York. Joined by CNN Legal Analyst and “Five Bullets: The Story of Bernie Goetz” Author Elliot Williams, Taylor Law Enforcement Consulting Group Roy Taylor, Representative Michael McCaul, JRepresentative Don Beyer, Former Biden Senior Advisor John McCarthy and Rising Communications Founder Leslie Shedd. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Sen. Tillis Expected to Clear Way for Warsh as Fed Chair
title_cn: 参议员蒂利斯有望为沃什担任美联储主席扫清道路
summary: Bloomberg's Michael McKee breaks down the big week ahead for the Fed, with Jerome Powell's final press conference on rate cuts this Wednesday as his term ends next month. Hours earlier, the Senate Banking Committee will move forward with confirming Kevin Warsh to be the next Fed Chair after the DOJ investigation was dropped. Sunday morning, Sen. Thom Tillis (R-North Carolina) said he would support Warsh's nomination. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: King Charles' Security Under Review ahead of DC State Visit
title_cn: 查尔斯国王的安全在华盛顿国事访问之前接受审查
summary: Buckingham Palace says it is discussing whether Saturday's shooting at the Washington Hilton will impact "operational planning" for King Charles' first State visit to the United States. The King and Queen are scheduled to meet with President Trump for a State Dinner at this White House this week before traveling to New York City. Bloomberg's Mishal Husain discusses the new security challenge and global reaction to the shooting in Washington on "Bloomberg This Weekend." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: History of Presidential Assassination Attempts
title_cn: 总统暗杀企图的历史
summary:
In the aftermath of the shooting at the White House Correspondents's dinner, Bloomberg's Joe Mathieu joins David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss the history of assassination attempts on US presidents how such events have shaped security protocols in Washington, DC.
 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: How Boring Businesses Create Billionaires
title_cn: 无聊的企业如何创造亿万富翁
summary: Forget tech startups and stock picks. A Princeton economist's research reveals that many of America's millionaires built their fortunes through private businesses in industries most people overlook. Billionaire Rich Kinder did it with pipelines. Ray and Dana Chery are hoping to become the next generation of billionaires by betting on portable sinks. Their stories challenge conventional wisdom on how wealth is actually built. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Investors Protecting Stocks at Record Eye Bets on Higher Rates
title_cn: 投资者以创纪录的目光押注更高利率来保护股票
summary: As stocks hit record after record, some investors are taking to heart the saying: Hedge when you can, not when you must.

title: Secret Service Likely to Lead Investigation into WHCD Shooting Suspect
title_cn: 特勤局可能会领导对 WHCD 枪击嫌疑人的调查
summary: Due to the involvement of presidential protection, the Secret Service, with the support of the FBI, is expected to lead the investigation into the suspected shooter. Former Federal, Local and State Police Office Roy Taylor and CNN Legal Analyst Elliot Williams discuss with David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend possible next steps. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Big Tech’s $16 Trillion Earnings Week Is Make-Or-Break for Rally

summary: Wall Street’s biggest technology stocks have carried the S&P 500 to record highs even as the war in Iran continues. Now, earnings from a handful of them this week will give investors a read on whether this rally’s sustainable.

title: Shots Fired at White House Correspondents' Dinner
title_cn: 白宫记者晚宴上发生枪击事件
summary: The suspect in the shooting during the White House Correspondents' Dinner has been identified as Cole Allen and is in custody. Bloomberg News White House Correspondent Jeff Mason and Journalist and Author Elliot Williams join David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss what is known and what still needs to be learned.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Trump Evacuated After Shooting at Press Dinner, Gunman Detained

summary: US authorities are charging a gunman in a shooting at the hotel that hosted the White House Correspondents’ Dinner in Washington, an incident that forced President Donald Trump to be rushed from the stage and the event to be postponed. Bloomberg News White House Correspondent Jeff Mason and State Department Reporter Eric Martin join David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Can the World’s Economic Firefighter Adapt to the 21st Century?
title_cn: 世界经济消防员能否适应21世纪？
summary: As wars, trade spats, and AI reshape the global economy, the IMF has held its role as the world’s lender-of-last-resort steady. Much of its governance, including countries’ shares of voting rights, have remained the same over decades, often requiring lending countries to return to the same austerity playbook. Gita Gopinath, former first managing director of the IMF says that the stability is by design in times of greater geopolitical uncertainty while Boston University’s Kevin Gallagher says that some parts of the IMF are due for an upgrade. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Musk Vies to Turn X Into Super App With Banking Tool Near Launch

President Donald Trump and Vice President JD Vance were evacuated from the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner in Washington on Saturday after shots were fired at the hotel hosting the event. A suspect is now in custody and being questioned, Trump said.

 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Trump’s Hormuz Blockade Has Deepened a Historic Shipping Crisis
title_cn: 特朗普的霍尔木兹封锁加深了历史性的航运危机
summary: In early April — after a month of disruption around one of the world’s most important energy chokepoints — US President Donald Trump wrote that with “a little ​more time, we can easily OPEN THE ​HORMUZ STRAIT, TAKE THE OIL, & MAKE A ⁠FORTUNE.”

title: China’s Tech-Heavy EVs Dominate Beijing’s Auto Show
title_cn: 中国科技含量高的电动汽车主导北京车展
summary: Legacy foreign automakers once held the keys to China’s car market, but the 2026 Beijing Auto Show reveals a dramatic power shift. From onboard cinemas to built-in toilets, domestic EV makers are winning over consumers with rapid-fire tech innovation and aggressive pricing. From the show floor in Beijing, Bloomberg’s Stephen Engle finds that as Western brands scramble to partner with local rivals to survive, China is now firmly in the driver’s seat. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Private-Sector Sleuthing Becomes Big Business for US Tech Startup

title: Understanding the Most Viral Chart in Artificial Intelligence | Odd Lots
title_cn: 了解人工智能中最热门的图表 |零股
summary:
METR, which stands for Model Evaluation and Threat Researc, is focused on understanding the degree to which AI models can engage in autonomous, complex tasks. METR see this is as a particularly important benchmark, given the risk that AI could one day be engaged in recursive self improvement, taking humans out of the loop. But how do you really gauge a model's ability to do complex problems. And what is being measured for exactly? On this episode we speak with METR's President Chris Painter as well as Joel Becker, a member of the technical staff who works on evaluation methods for the organization. We discuss both the mechanics and the philosophy of METR's work, and what it means when we see a a chart showing that Clause Opus 4.6 can do a task that would take a human nearly 12 hours.
 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Graticule’s Levinson ‘Open’ to Iran Resolution Ahead of Rally
title_cn: Graticule 的莱文森在集会前对伊朗决议持“开放”态度
summary: Investor Adam Levinson said he remains bullish despite recent market volatility, and is buying the dip ahead of an expected rally on a resolution in the Iran conflict.

title: China Says US Export Bills Risk Disrupting Chip Supply Chains
title_cn: 中国称美国出口票据有扰乱芯片供应链的风险
summary: China said it is closely monitoring US legislative plans after the House Foreign Affairs Committee advanced export-control bills targeting semiconductors, which Beijing warned could disrupt global supply chains if passed.

title: Denso to Withdraw Proposal to Take Over Rohm, Nikkei Says

title: DC Gala Gunman Believed to Be Targeting US Officials
title_cn: DC Gala 枪手据信目标是美国官员
summary: The armed suspect who tried to enter a Washington ballroom where President Donald Trump was set to speak was likely targeting administration officials, Acting US Attorney General Todd Blanche said. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Trump Says Gala Attack Shows Security Need for His Ballroom
title_cn: 特朗普表示，晚会袭击表明他的宴会厅需要安全保障
summary: President Donald Trump jumped on Saturday night’s shooting at the White House Correspondents’ Dinner to add a security rationale to his case for building a massive White House ballroom that he has wanted for years.

title: Chevron CEO Says Venezuela Must Do More for Oil Industry Revival
title_cn: 雪佛龙首席执行官表示委内瑞拉必须为石油工业复兴做出更多努力
summary: Chevron Corp. Chief Executive Officer Mike Wirth said changes to Venezuela’s oil policy are a sign of progress in trying to attract foreign investment, though further measures are needed.

title: Another Attack at ‘Hinckley Hilton’ Raises New Security Concerns
title_cn: “欣克利希尔顿”的另一次袭击引发了新的安全担忧
summary: An attempted attack at Saturday’s White House Correspondents’ Dinner is spurring fresh questions about the security around President Donald Trump and other top officials.

title: Gunman in DC Gala Attack Built Up Arsenal to Target US Officials
title_cn: 华盛顿晚会袭击中的枪手建立了武器库以瞄准美国官员
summary: The man accused of storming the White House Correspondents’ Dinner spent years quietly acquiring his arsenal, purchasing a shotgun from a Torrance, California, firearms dealer eight months before the attack and a semi-automatic pistol two years earlier, according to a law enforcement intelligence profile reviewed by Bloomberg.

title: Ex-Israeli Prime Ministers Unite Parties to Take On Netanyahu
title_cn: 以色列前总理联合各方对抗内塔尼亚胡
summary: Naftali Bennett and Yair Lapid, two former prime ministers of Israel, announced the merging of their parties, creating a new political entity that could outpoll that of Benjamin Netanyahu’s Likud in the upcoming election.

title: Rep. Beyer: "We Are More Divided Than We've Ever Been" Following DC Shooting
title_cn: 众议员拜尔：华盛顿枪击事件后“我们比以往任何时候都更加分裂”
summary: Rep. Don Beyer (D-Virginia) talks to "Bloomberg This Weekend" about his concerns following the shooting at the White House Correspondents' Dinner and the redistricting vote in Virginia that could tilt the balance in the Midterm elections. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Rep. McCaul on How to Combat Political Violence
title_cn: 众议员麦考尔谈如何打击政治暴力
summary: Rep. Michael McCaul (R-Texas) shares his experience with David Gura and Christina Ruffini as a guest at the White House Correspondents' Dinner, where shots were fired outside the ballroom. He tells "Bloomberg This Weekend" what needs to be done to tone down political rhetoric following another shooting targeting President Trump. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US-Iran Peace Talks Stall as Conflict Nears Two-Month Mark
title_cn: 冲突接近两个月，美伊和平谈判陷入僵局
summary: Efforts to resume peace talks over the Iran war stalled after US President Donald Trump canceled a planned trip by his top envoys and the Islamic Republic said it won’t negotiate so long as it’s being threatened.

title: Gunman at DC Gala Believed to Be Targeting US Officials
title_cn: DC Gala上的枪手被认为是针对美国官员
summary: The armed suspect who tried to enter a Washington ballroom where President Donald Trump was speaking was likely targeting administration officials, Acting US Attorney General Todd Blanche said.

title: Gunman at DC Gala Sought to Assassinate Trump, Leavitt Says
title_cn: 莱维特称，华盛顿晚会上的枪手试图刺杀特朗普
summary: White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt described the gunman at the White House Correspondents’ Dinner as a “crazy person who sought to assassinate the President and kill as many top Trump administration officials as possible.”

title: Australia Adds to Weapons Buildup With New Armored Vehicle Deal
title_cn: 澳大利亚通过新的装甲车交易增加武器建设
summary: Australia said it will spend more than $800 million to buy armored vehicles, adding to its recent spurt of weapons purchases as the government implements its biggest peacetime increase in defense spending.

title: Incoming Hungary PM, Von der Leyen to Discuss Funds on Wednesday
title_cn: 即将上任的匈牙利总理冯德莱恩将于周三讨论资金问题
summary: Hungary’s incoming Prime Minister Peter Magyar will visit Brussels on Wednesday for talks with European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen on resuming flows of EU funding, he posted on social media.

title: Israel President Holds Off Deciding Whether to Pardon Netanyahu
title_cn: 以色列总统推迟决定是否赦免内塔尼亚胡
summary: Israeli President Isaac Herzog has decided to shelve, for now, Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s request for a pardon in his graft trial, preferring to press the parties to reach an agreement that would terminate the case consensually, his office announced.

title: Ukraine Attacks Russia Fertilizer Plant and Oil Refinery
title_cn: 乌克兰袭击俄罗斯化肥厂和炼油厂
summary: Ukrainian drones caused damage to a fertilizer plant owned by PhosAgro PJSC as well as a major oil refinery as Kyiv seeks to thwart Russian commodities exporters which are benefiting from higher prices as a result of the Iran war.

title: A British King Tries to Stop Another Historic Break With America
title_cn: 英国国王试图阻止与美国的又一次历史性决裂
summary: To say the special relationship is going through a rough patch ahead of King Charles III’s visit would be an exercise in British understatement.

title: Australia’s Wong Heads to Japan, China and Korea to Talk Energy
title_cn: 澳大利亚黄前往日本、中国和韩国讨论能源问题
summary: Australian Foreign Minister Penny Wong will head to Japan, China and South Korea this week to talk energy supplies, ahead of the Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi’s expected visit to the continent in early May.

title: Starmer Stays Defiant as Mandelson Saga Continues to Swirl
title_cn: 曼德尔森传奇继续发酵，斯塔默保持挑衅态度
summary: Prime Minister Keir Starmer insists he’ll remain in the job and lead the Labour Party into the next elections, even as the controversy over his appointment of Peter Mandelson as ambassador to the US refuses to die down.

title: Israeli Cabinet Approves Appointment of Ambassador to Somaliland
title_cn: 以色列内阁批准任命驻索马里兰大使
summary: Benjamin Netanyahu’s cabinet approved the appointment of the Israel’s first ever ambassador to Somaliland, a breakaway region of Somalia, the Foreign Ministry said in a statement on Sunday.

title: Labour’s Lowest Ebb in Century in Wales Poses Peril for Starmer
title_cn: 威尔士工党百年来最低潮给斯塔默带来危险
summary: Keir Starmer’s Labour Party is projected to lose seats nationwide in UK local and regional elections on May 7.  Nowhere will that sting more than in the race for the Welsh Senedd.

title: Armed Man Stormed a White House Press Dinner Checkpoint
title_cn: 武装男子冲进白宫新闻晚宴检查站
summary: A man carrying a shotgun, a handgun and multiple knives charged a Secret Service checkpoint at the Washington Hilton Saturday night, wounding a federal officer and forcing President Donald Trump and members of his cabinet to flee the ballroom of the White House Correspondents’ Dinner.

title: Gunman Detained, Trump Evacuated After Shooting at Press Dinner
title_cn: 枪手被捕，特朗普在新闻晚宴上枪击后撤离
summary: US authorities are charging a gunman in a shooting at the hotel that hosted the White House correspondents’ dinner in Washington, an incident that forced President Donald Trump to be rushed from the stage and the event to be postponed.

title: DC Media Gala With Trump Plunged Into Chaos When Shots Rang Out
title_cn: 特朗普的华盛顿媒体晚会在枪声响起时陷入混乱
summary: Oz Pearlman, a mentalist who was headlining the annual White House Correspondents’ Dinner, was performing a trick, trying to guess the name of the baby Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt is expecting, when loud popping noises rang out.

title: Trump Says Suspect a ‘Lone Wolf,’ Investigation Underway
title_cn: 特朗普称嫌疑人是“独狼”，调查正在进行中
summary: President Donald Trump said his impression is that the suspect who disrupted Saturday’s White House Correspondents’ Association dinner in Washington, DC, acted as a “lone wolf” and was in custody as law enforcement investigates the incident.

title: Trump Recounts Shooting Incident at Correspondents’ Dinner
title_cn: 特朗普在记者晚宴上讲述枪击事件
summary: President Donald Trump makes a statement after being evacuated from the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner event in Washington Saturday after shots were fired near the venue. Trump said a suspect is in custody and one law enforcement officer was shot, but that the officer’s bulletproof vest did its job. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Trump Evacuated After Shots Fired at DC Event
title_cn: 特朗普在华盛顿活动中发生枪击事件后撤离
summary: President Donald Trump and Vice President JD Vance were evacuated from the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner event in Washington Saturday following a security incident at the venue. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Trump, Vance Evacuated From DC Event After Shots Fired
title_cn: 特朗普和万斯在枪击事件后从华盛顿特区的活动中撤离
summary: President Donald Trump and Vice President JD Vance were evacuated from the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner event in Washington Saturday following a security incident at the venue. (Source: Bloomberg)
